templates.description: Armor stone sizing using Hudson's equation W = rho_r * H^3 / (KD * (Sr-1)^3 * cot(alpha))
templates.long_description: Determines the required median armor stone weight and nominal diameter for breakwater and revetment stability using Hudson's (1959) formula from the USACE Coastal Engineering Manual and CIRIA C683 Rock Manual. Accounts for design wave height, rock and water densities, structure slope, and the stability coefficient KD which depends on armor type and placement method.

inputs.name: design_wave_height_m
inputs.description: Design wave height H

inputs.name: rock_density_kg_m3
inputs.description: Armor rock density rho_r

inputs.name: water_density_kg_m3
inputs.description: Water density rho_w

inputs.name: slope_angle_deg
inputs.description: Structure slope angle alpha from horizontal

inputs.name: stability_coefficient_kd
inputs.description: Hudson stability coefficient KD

outputs.name: specific_gravity_sr
outputs.description: Specific gravity of rock Sr = rho_r / rho_w

templates.description: Freeboard calculation for coastal/flood structures: total freeboard = wave_allowance + slr_allowance + construction_tolerance + safety_margin (NZS 4404 / MfE Guidance)
templates.long_description: Determines the total freeboard and minimum crest or floor level for coastal and flood-prone structures by summing component allowances for wave overtopping, sea level rise, construction tolerance, and a consequence-based safety margin, per NZS 4404:2010 and MfE Guidance 2024. Used in coastal and floodplain development to set minimum building levels above the design water surface.

inputs.name: design_water_level_m
inputs.description: Design still-water level including tidal and storm surge components (m above datum)

inputs.name: wave_allowance_m
inputs.description: Wave overtopping allowance depending on wave height and structure type

inputs.name: slr_allowance_m
inputs.description: Climate change sea level rise allowance for planning horizon

inputs.name: construction_tolerance_m
inputs.description: Construction tolerance allowance

inputs.name: safety_margin_m
inputs.description: Safety margin based on consequence category

outputs.name: minimum_crest_level_m
outputs.description: Minimum crest or floor level (m above datum)

templates.description: Culvert headwater analysis under inlet and outlet control using HDS-5 methodology
templates.long_description: Determines the headwater depth for circular culverts under both inlet control and outlet control conditions per the FHWA HDS-5 methodology, then identifies the controlling condition. Inlet control uses regression-based unsubmerged/submerged equations while outlet control applies an energy balance with entrance, friction, and exit losses. Used in road drainage design to verify that headwater elevations remain within acceptable limits.

inputs.name: culvert_diameter_m
inputs.description: Internal culvert diameter D

inputs.name: culvert_length_m
inputs.description: Culvert barrel length L

inputs.name: culvert_slope_m_per_m
inputs.description: Culvert barrel slope S

inputs.name: design_flow_m3_s
inputs.description: Design discharge Q through the culvert

inputs.name: culvert_configuration
inputs.description: Culvert material and inlet type combination

inputs.name: tailwater_depth_m
inputs.description: Tailwater depth above outlet invert TW

inputs.name: invert_elevation_m
inputs.description: Inlet invert elevation

outputs.name: inlet_control_hw_m
outputs.description: Headwater depth above inlet invert under inlet control (m)

outputs.name: outlet_control_hw_m
outputs.description: Headwater depth above inlet invert under outlet control (m)

outputs.name: controlling_condition
outputs.description: Controlling condition: 1.0 = inlet control, 2.0 = outlet control

outputs.name: headwater_elevation_m
outputs.description: Headwater elevation at controlling condition (m AHD)

templates.description: Preliminary detention volume estimate using simplified triangular hydrograph method
templates.long_description: Estimates the required stormwater detention storage volume using a simplified triangular inflow hydrograph with constant outflow, as commonly applied in preliminary site drainage design per TR-55 and local council requirements. Computes the volume difference between post-development peak inflow and the allowable release rate over the storm duration, and derives an approximate basin surface area from a nominated design depth.

inputs.name: post_dev_peak_flow_m3_s
inputs.description: Post-development peak flow rate Q_post

inputs.name: allowable_release_rate_m3_s
inputs.description: Allowable (pre-development or regulated) release rate Q_allow

inputs.name: storm_duration_hr
inputs.description: Design storm duration t_storm

inputs.name: design_depth_m
inputs.description: Design water depth of the detention basin

outputs.name: approximate_surface_area_m2
outputs.description: Approximate basin surface area at design depth (m²)

templates.description: Size orifice outlet to achieve target release rate from detention basin
templates.long_description: Sizes a circular orifice outlet for a stormwater detention basin by rearranging the orifice equation Q = Cd*A*sqrt(2gH) to solve for the required orifice area and diameter given a target discharge flow rate and available head. Commonly applied in urban stormwater management to control post-development peak flows.

inputs.name: design_flow_m3_s
inputs.description: Target discharge flow rate Q

inputs.name: head_above_centreline_m
inputs.description: Head of water above orifice centreline H

inputs.name: discharge_coefficient
inputs.description: Orifice discharge coefficient Cd

templates.description: Size emergency spillway weir for detention basin overflow
templates.long_description: Sizes a sharp-crested rectangular weir for detention basin emergency overflow using the Francis formula Q = Cw * (L - 0.1*n*H) * H^1.5, where Cw = Cd * sqrt(2g). Solves for the required crest length given design flow, head, discharge coefficient, and end contractions, supporting stormwater detention and flood control design.

inputs.name: design_flow_m3_s
inputs.description: Design overflow discharge Q

inputs.name: head_over_weir_m
inputs.description: Head of water over the weir crest H

inputs.name: discharge_coefficient
inputs.description: Weir discharge coefficient Cd

inputs.name: number_of_contractions
inputs.description: Number of end contractions n (0 = suppressed, 1 or 2 = contracted)

outputs.name: unit_discharge_m3_s_per_m
outputs.description: Unit discharge per metre of weir crest q (m³/s/m)

templates.description: Driveway gradient calculation and compliance check per AS/NZS 2890.1:2004
templates.long_description: Calculates the driveway gradient as a percentage from the level difference and horizontal distance, then checks compliance against location-specific maximum gradients defined in AS/NZS 2890.1:2004 and typical Australian council development control plans. Covers transition zones, residential and commercial internal sections, garage approaches, and shared pedestrian/vehicle paths.

inputs.name: start_level_m
inputs.description: Start level of the driveway section (reduced level)

inputs.name: end_level_m
inputs.description: End level of the driveway section (reduced level)

inputs.name: horizontal_length_m
inputs.description: Horizontal distance of the driveway section

inputs.name: location_type
inputs.description: Driveway section location type determining the maximum allowable gradient

outputs.name: max_allowable_gradient_pct
outputs.description: Maximum allowable gradient for the location type (%)

outputs.name: compliance
outputs.description: Compliance with maximum gradient (1.0 = pass, 0.0 = fail)

templates.description: Size construction sediment basin per Blue Book (Soils and Construction)
templates.long_description: Sizes construction-phase sediment basins for erosion and sediment control per the Blue Book (Managing Urban Stormwater: Soils and Construction). Calculates settling zone volume (V_s = Cv*A), sediment storage volume (V_sed = R*A*D), and total basin volume for Type D (dry) or Type F (wet with permanent pool) configurations based on soil loss rates and climate region.
standards: Managing Urban Stormwater: Soils and Construction (Blue Book)

inputs.name: catchment_area_ha
inputs.description: Contributing catchment area A

inputs.name: volumetric_runoff_coeff_m3_ha
inputs.description: Volumetric runoff coefficient Cv (varies by rainfall region)

inputs.name: soil_loss_rate_m3_ha_yr
inputs.description: Soil loss rate R

inputs.name: cleanout_interval_yr
inputs.description: Sediment clean-out interval D

inputs.name: basin_type
inputs.description: Basin type: D (dry) or F (wet with permanent pool)

inputs.name: permanent_pool_volume_m3
inputs.description: Permanent pool volume for Type F basins

templates.description: Gravity sewer pipe sizing using Manning's equation to select standard diameter for design flow
templates.long_description: Selects the smallest standard gravity sewer pipe diameter (per WSAA WSA 02 / AS 4130) that conveys a given design flow using Manning's equation for full-pipe capacity. Computes pipe slope from invert levels, iterates standard diameters from 150 mm to 1200 mm, and reports the full-pipe velocity and flow depth ratio for the selected pipe.

inputs.name: design_flow_l_s
inputs.description: Peak design flow entering the pipe

inputs.name: upstream_invert_m
inputs.description: Upstream pipe invert elevation

inputs.name: downstream_invert_m
inputs.description: Downstream pipe invert elevation

inputs.name: pipe_length_m
inputs.description: Pipe length between manholes

inputs.name: mannings_n
inputs.description: Manning's roughness coefficient n

outputs.name: flow_depth_ratio
outputs.description: Approximate flow depth ratio d/D (design flow / full capacity)

templates.description: Gravity sewer slope adequacy check for self-cleansing velocity using Manning's equation
templates.long_description: Verifies that a gravity sewer pipe's installed slope produces a full-pipe velocity within the self-cleansing range (0.6-4.0 m/s) required by WSAA WSA 02. Uses Manning's equation V = (1/n)*R_h^(2/3)*S^(1/2) for a circular pipe at full flow to compute velocity and capacity, then reports compliance status.

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_mm
inputs.description: Internal pipe diameter D

inputs.name: pipe_slope_pct
inputs.description: Pipe longitudinal slope as a percentage

inputs.name: mannings_n
inputs.description: Manning's roughness coefficient n

templates.description: Horizontal curve element calculation: tangent, arc, external distance, mid-ordinate, and PC/PT chainages
templates.long_description: Computes the geometric elements of a simple horizontal circular curve given the radius, deflection angle, and intersection-point chainage, per AGRD Part 3. Derives tangent length (T = R tan(delta/2)), arc length, external distance, mid-ordinate, and PC/PT chainages. Used in road and rail alignment design to set out curve geometry in the field.

inputs.name: curve_radius_m
inputs.description: Horizontal curve radius R

inputs.name: deflection_angle_deg
inputs.description: Deflection (intersection) angle Δ between tangents

inputs.name: ip_chainage_m
inputs.description: Chainage of the intersection point (IP)

outputs.name: pc_chainage_m
outputs.description: Chainage of the point of curvature PC (m)

outputs.name: pt_chainage_m
outputs.description: Chainage of the point of tangency PT (m)

templates.description: Minimum horizontal curve radius using R = V^2 / (127 * (e_max + f)) per AGRD Part 3 §7
templates.long_description: Calculates the absolute minimum and desirable minimum horizontal curve radius for road design using R_min = V^2 / (127 * (e_max + f)) per Austroads Guide to Road Design Part 3 Section 7. The desirable radius uses a reduced friction factor (0.7f) for an additional safety margin. Used in road geometric design to ensure vehicle stability and driver comfort on horizontal curves.

inputs.name: design_speed_km_h
inputs.description: Design speed V

inputs.name: max_superelevation_pct
inputs.description: Maximum superelevation rate e_max

inputs.name: side_friction_factor
inputs.description: Side friction factor f (speed-dependent, from AGRD Table 7.5)

outputs.name: min_radius_m
outputs.description: Absolute minimum horizontal curve radius R_min (m)

outputs.name: desirable_min_radius_m
outputs.description: Desirable minimum horizontal curve radius R_desirable (m)

templates.description: Superelevation rate and development length for horizontal curves using e + f = V^2 / (127 * R)
templates.long_description: Determines the required superelevation rate and transition development length for horizontal road curves using the point-mass equilibrium equation e + f = V^2/(127*R) per AASHTO Green Book and Austroads AGRD Part 3 Section 7.5. Balances centripetal force demand between pavement banking and tyre side friction to ensure vehicle stability through curves.

inputs.name: design_speed_km_h
inputs.description: Design speed V

inputs.name: curve_radius_m
inputs.description: Horizontal curve radius R

inputs.name: side_friction_factor
inputs.description: Side friction factor f

inputs.name: lane_width_m
inputs.description: Lane width w

inputs.name: rotation_rate
inputs.description: Maximum rate of pavement rotation (e.g. 1/200 = 0.005)

templates.description: Flow capacity and velocity in circular pipes using Manning's equation
templates.long_description: Computes the flow capacity, velocity, flow area, and hydraulic radius of circular drainage pipes using Manning's equation Q = (1/n) * A * R^(2/3) * S^(1/2), supporting both full and partially full pipe flow via the central angle geometry method. Used in stormwater and sewer pipe sizing per HEC-22 and QUDM to verify that pipe capacity meets the design discharge.

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_m
inputs.description: Internal pipe diameter D

inputs.name: mannings_n
inputs.description: Manning's roughness coefficient n

inputs.name: slope_m_per_m
inputs.description: Longitudinal pipe slope S

inputs.name: flow_depth_ratio
inputs.description: Flow depth to diameter ratio d/D (1.0 = full)

templates.description: Flow capacity and velocity in trapezoidal/rectangular open channels using Manning's equation
templates.long_description: Computes flow area, wetted perimeter, hydraulic radius, velocity, discharge capacity, and Froude number for trapezoidal or rectangular open channels using Manning's equation V = (1/n)*R^(2/3)*S^(1/2). Used in drainage and flood conveyance design per HEC-22 and Australian Rainfall and Runoff (ARR) standards.

inputs.name: bottom_width_m
inputs.description: Channel bottom width b

inputs.name: flow_depth_m
inputs.description: Flow depth y

inputs.name: side_slope_z
inputs.description: Side slope ratio z (horizontal:vertical, 0 for rectangular)

inputs.name: mannings_n
inputs.description: Manning's roughness coefficient n

inputs.name: channel_slope_m_per_m
inputs.description: Longitudinal channel slope S

templates.description: Roadway gutter spread width and curb depth from HEC-22 Manning's equation for triangular cross-sections
templates.long_description: Determines the width of water spread across a roadway pavement and curb flow depth using the HEC-22 Manning's equation for triangular gutter sections: T = (Q*n / (K_u * Sx^(5/3) * S_L^(1/2)))^(3/8). Used for inlet spacing design and pavement drainage adequacy checks to ensure roadway safety during design storms.

inputs.name: gutter_flow_m3_s
inputs.description: Design gutter flow rate Q

inputs.name: cross_slope_pct
inputs.description: Roadway cross-slope Sx (percentage)

inputs.name: longitudinal_slope_pct
inputs.description: Longitudinal road gradient S_L (percentage)

inputs.name: mannings_n
inputs.description: Manning's roughness coefficient n for the pavement surface

outputs.name: spread_width_m
outputs.description: Width of water spread on the roadway T (m)

outputs.name: curb_depth_m
outputs.description: Flow depth at the curb face d (m)

templates.description: Peak runoff using the rational method Q = CIA / 360
templates.long_description: Computes peak stormwater runoff discharge using the rational method formula Q = C*I*A/360, where C is the runoff coefficient, I is the design rainfall intensity, and A is the catchment area. Applicable to small catchments up to 80 hectares per ARR and HEC-22, widely used for sizing drainage infrastructure in urban and rural settings.

inputs.name: runoff_coefficient
inputs.description: Runoff coefficient C (dimensionless)

inputs.name: rainfall_intensity_mm_hr
inputs.description: Design rainfall intensity I

inputs.name: catchment_area_ha
inputs.description: Catchment area A

templates.description: Runoff depth using the SCS/NRCS curve number method Q = (P - Ia)^2 / (P - Ia + S)
templates.long_description: Calculates storm runoff depth from rainfall using the SCS/NRCS curve number method per TR-55. Derives potential maximum retention S = (25400/CN) - 254 and initial abstraction Ia = 0.2*S, then computes excess rainfall as Q = (P - Ia)^2 / (P - Ia + S). Widely used for hydrologic modelling of ungauged catchments based on soil type and land cover.

inputs.name: rainfall_depth_mm
inputs.description: Total storm rainfall depth P

inputs.name: curve_number
inputs.description: SCS/NRCS curve number CN (dimensionless)

templates.description: Generate and check serviceability limit state load combinations per AS/NZS 1170.0 Table 4.1
templates.long_description: Computes serviceability limit state load combinations for structural deflection checks using AS/NZS 1170.0 Table 4.1. Determines short-term and long-term combination factors from the imposed-action category, applies them to dead and live loads, adds wind serviceability actions where applicable, and identifies the governing SLS combination for design.

inputs.name: dead_load_kn
inputs.description: Permanent (dead) load G acting on the member

inputs.name: live_load_kn
inputs.description: Imposed (live) load Q acting on the member

inputs.name: wind_serviceability_kn
inputs.description: Serviceability wind action W_s on the member (zero if wind not applicable)

inputs.name: load_category
inputs.description: Imposed-action category per AS 1170.1 (A = domestic, B = offices, C = public assembly, D = retail, E = storage)

outputs.name: psi_s
outputs.description: Short-term combination factor from AS/NZS 1170.0 Table 4.1

outputs.name: psi_l
outputs.description: Long-term combination factor from AS/NZS 1170.0 Table 4.1

outputs.name: sls_short_term_kn
outputs.description: Short-term SLS combination G + psi_s * Q (kN)

outputs.name: sls_long_term_kn
outputs.description: Long-term SLS combination G + psi_l * Q (kN)

outputs.name: sls_wind_kn
outputs.description: Wind SLS combination G + psi_s * Q + W_s (kN)

templates.description: Generate and check ultimate limit state load combinations per AS/NZS 1170.0 Table 4.1
templates.long_description: Computes all five ULS load combinations from AS/NZS 1170.0 Table 4.1 for a structural element subject to permanent, imposed, wind, and earthquake actions. Applies the appropriate combination factors (psi_c and psi_E) based on the imposed action category, then identifies the governing design action for strength design.

inputs.name: dead_load_kn
inputs.description: Permanent (dead) load G

inputs.name: live_load_kn
inputs.description: Imposed (live) load Q

inputs.name: wind_ultimate_kn
inputs.description: Ultimate wind action W_u

inputs.name: earthquake_load_kn
inputs.description: Earthquake action E

inputs.name: load_category
inputs.description: Imposed action category per AS/NZS 1170.0 (A-D general, E storage)

outputs.name: uls_wind_kn
outputs.description: ULS combination 3: 1.2 * G + psi_c * Q + W_u (kN)

outputs.name: uls_earthquake_kn
outputs.description: ULS combination 5: G + psi_E * Q + E (kN)

outputs.name: governing_uls_kn
outputs.description: Governing ULS design action Ed = max of all combinations (kN)

templates.description: Oil containment bund volume calculation per AS/NZS 1940
templates.long_description: Calculates the required bund containment capacity for oil and hazardous liquid storage as the greater of 110% of the largest container or 25% of total stored volume, per AS/NZS 1940. Accounts for equipment displacement within the bund and checks whether the net available volume meets the regulatory minimum.

inputs.name: num_containers
inputs.description: Number of oil-filled containers within the bund

inputs.name: largest_container_volume_l
inputs.description: Volume of the largest single container

inputs.name: total_stored_volume_l
inputs.description: Total stored volume across all containers

inputs.name: bund_length_m
inputs.description: Internal length of the bund enclosure

inputs.name: bund_width_m
inputs.description: Internal width of the bund enclosure

inputs.name: bund_wall_height_m
inputs.description: Height of the bund containment walls

inputs.name: num_equipment_items
inputs.description: Number of equipment items inside the bund (pumps, pipework supports, etc.)

inputs.name: equipment_footprint_area_m2
inputs.description: Average footprint area of each equipment item inside the bund

outputs.name: net_bund_volume_m3
outputs.description: Net available bund volume after equipment displacement (m³)

outputs.name: compliance
outputs.description: Compliance flag: 1.0 if net >= required, 0.0 otherwise

templates.description: Flap gate headloss calculation for stormwater outfalls
templates.long_description: Calculates energy loss through flap gates (tide gates / non-return valves) installed on stormwater outfalls using the orifice equation h = V^2/(2gCd^2). Determines the discharge coefficient from gate type, looks up unseating head from pipe diameter, and computes the effective capacity reduction compared to an open pipe. Used in drainage design to verify that outfall losses remain within acceptable limits for the catchment.

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_mm
inputs.description: Nominal pipe diameter at the outfall

inputs.name: flow_velocity_m_per_s
inputs.description: Mean flow velocity in the outfall pipe V

inputs.name: gate_type
inputs.description: Flap gate hinge configuration

inputs.name: upstream_head_m
inputs.description: Upstream head driving flow through the gate

outputs.name: headloss_m
outputs.description: Energy loss through the flap gate (m)

outputs.name: unseating_head_m
outputs.description: Minimum head to open the gate against its own weight (m)

outputs.name: capacity_reduction_percent
outputs.description: Effective capacity reduction compared to open pipe (%)

outputs.name: discharge_coefficient
outputs.description: Discharge coefficient Cd for the gate type

templates.description: Outfall submergence analysis: fraction of tidal cycle an outfall invert is submerged under present and future sea levels
templates.long_description: Determines the percentage of time a stormwater or wastewater outfall is submerged by tidal waters under present-day conditions and a future sea level rise scenario. Uses a sinusoidal tide model h(t) = MSL + A sin(2 pi t / T) with a closed-form solution for the submerged fraction, then converts to hours per day. Applicable to coastal outfall feasibility assessments per MfE Coastal Hazards Guidance 2024 and Australian coastal design practice.

inputs.name: outfall_invert_level_m
inputs.description: Outfall pipe invert level relative to datum (m AHD or m CD)

inputs.name: mean_sea_level_m
inputs.description: Present-day mean sea level relative to datum

inputs.name: tidal_amplitude_m
inputs.description: Tidal amplitude (half the spring tidal range), A = (HAT - LAT) / 2

inputs.name: sea_level_rise_m
inputs.description: Projected sea level rise for the design planning horizon

inputs.name: tidal_period_hours
inputs.description: Tidal period (typically 12.42 hours for semi-diurnal M2 constituent)

outputs.name: present_submergence_percent
outputs.description: Percentage of time outfall is submerged under present-day conditions

outputs.name: future_submergence_percent
outputs.description: Percentage of time outfall is submerged under future sea level conditions

outputs.name: present_hours_submerged_per_day
outputs.description: Hours per day the outfall is submerged (present-day)

outputs.name: future_hours_submerged_per_day
outputs.description: Hours per day the outfall is submerged (future)

outputs.name: submergence_increase_percent
outputs.description: Absolute increase in submergence percentage from SLR

templates.description: Friction head loss calculation using Darcy-Weisbach equation with Swamee-Jain friction factor
templates.long_description: Calculates friction head loss in pressurised pipe flow using the Darcy-Weisbach equation hf = f * (L/D) * V^2/(2g), with the friction factor determined by the Swamee-Jain explicit approximation for turbulent flow or f = 64/Re for laminar flow. Computes Reynolds number and flow velocity as intermediate results. Used in water supply and hydraulic pipeline design to size pipes and evaluate pressure losses.

inputs.name: flow_rate_m3_s
inputs.description: Volumetric flow rate Q

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_m
inputs.description: Internal pipe diameter D

inputs.name: pipe_length_m
inputs.description: Pipe length L

inputs.name: roughness_height_mm
inputs.description: Absolute roughness height epsilon

inputs.name: kinematic_viscosity_m2_s
inputs.description: Kinematic viscosity of the fluid nu

templates.description: Friction head loss calculation using the Hazen-Williams empirical equation
templates.long_description: Calculates friction head loss in pressurised water mains using the Hazen-Williams empirical formula hf = 10.67 * L * Q^1.852 / (C^1.852 * D^4.87), along with the hydraulic gradient and flow velocity. The C-factor encodes pipe material and condition. Widely used in municipal water distribution design per AWWA and AS/NZS 3500 for sizing pipes and evaluating network pressure losses.

inputs.name: flow_rate_l_s
inputs.description: Volumetric flow rate Q

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_mm
inputs.description: Internal pipe diameter D

inputs.name: pipe_length_m
inputs.description: Pipe length L

inputs.name: c_factor
inputs.description: Hazen-Williams roughness coefficient C

templates.description: Pipe flow velocity compliance check against AS/NZS 3500.1 service-type limits
templates.long_description: Calculates full-bore pipe flow velocity from V = Q/A and checks compliance against minimum and maximum velocity limits specified in AS/NZS 3500.1 for water supply, gravity sewer, stormwater, and fire service pipes. Ensures velocities remain within self-cleansing and scour-prevention bounds for the given service type.

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_mm
inputs.description: Internal pipe diameter D

inputs.name: flow_rate_l_s
inputs.description: Volumetric flow rate Q

inputs.name: service_type
inputs.description: Pipe service type per AS/NZS 3500.1

outputs.name: compliance
outputs.description: Velocity compliance (1.0 = within limits, 0.0 = outside limits)

templates.description: Net Positive Suction Head Available calculation for pump station suction systems
templates.long_description: Calculates the Net Positive Suction Head Available (NPSHa) at a pump inlet from atmospheric pressure, vapour pressure, static suction head, and friction losses using the Hydraulics Institute formula NPSHa = (P_atm - P_vap)/(rho*g) + h_s - h_f. Determines the NPSH margin and margin ratio against the pump's required NPSHr to verify cavitation-free operation per ANSI/HI 9.6.1.

inputs.name: atmospheric_pressure_kpa
inputs.description: Atmospheric pressure at the site elevation

inputs.name: vapour_pressure_kpa
inputs.description: Vapour pressure of the fluid at pumping temperature

inputs.name: specific_gravity
inputs.description: Specific gravity of the fluid relative to water

inputs.name: static_suction_head_m
inputs.description: Static suction head (positive = pump below liquid surface, negative = pump above)

inputs.name: friction_loss_m
inputs.description: Total friction losses in the suction piping

inputs.name: npsh_required_m
inputs.description: NPSH required by the pump (from manufacturer data)

outputs.name: pressure_head_m
outputs.description: Net pressure head contribution (P_atm - P_vap) / (rho * g) (m)

outputs.name: npsh_available_m
outputs.description: Net Positive Suction Head Available NPSHa (m)

templates.description: Pump power calculation for water/wastewater pump stations at a given duty point
templates.long_description: Calculates hydraulic power (P_h = rho*g*Q*H), brake (shaft) power, and motor input power for a water or wastewater pump station at a specified duty point. Accounts for pump and motor efficiencies to determine the electrical power draw, following AWWA and Hydraulics Institute methodology for pump station design.

inputs.name: flow_rate_l_s
inputs.description: Volumetric flow rate Q at the duty point

inputs.name: total_dynamic_head_m
inputs.description: Total dynamic head H (static head plus friction losses)

inputs.name: pump_efficiency_pct
inputs.description: Pump efficiency at the duty point

inputs.name: motor_efficiency_pct
inputs.description: Motor efficiency at the operating load

templates.description: Rail thermal stress and force in CWR using sigma = E * alpha * dT (AREMA / ARTC)
templates.long_description: Computes the longitudinal thermal stress and force in continuously welded rail (CWR) using sigma = E * alpha * delta_T. Critical for managing rail buckling risk in hot conditions and rail pull-apart risk in cold conditions, per AREMA Chapter 5 and ARTC ETS-05-00.

inputs.name: rail_area_mm2
inputs.description: Rail cross-sectional area A

inputs.name: thermal_expansion_coeff_micro_per_c
inputs.description: Coefficient of linear thermal expansion α

inputs.name: elastic_modulus_mpa
inputs.description: Modulus of elasticity E of rail steel

inputs.name: temperature_change_c
inputs.description: Temperature change from neutral temperature ΔT = T_rail − T_neutral

templates.description: Longshore sediment transport rate using the CERC formula Q_l = K * (E*Cg)_b * sin(2*alpha_b) / (2 * (rho_s - rho_w) * g * (1 - p))
templates.long_description: Estimates annual volumetric longshore sediment transport using the CERC formula from the USACE Coastal Engineering Manual and Shore Protection Manual. Calculates wave energy flux at the breaker line and converts it to a sediment transport rate, accounting for wave angle, sediment and water densities, and porosity. Used in coastal engineering to predict littoral drift and inform beach nourishment or breakwater design.

inputs.name: breaking_wave_height_m
inputs.description: Significant breaking wave height H_b

inputs.name: wave_angle_at_breaking_deg
inputs.description: Wave angle at breaking relative to shore-normal alpha_b (positive = left-to-right looking shoreward)

inputs.name: k_coefficient
inputs.description: CERC empirical transport coefficient K

inputs.name: sediment_density_kg_m3
inputs.description: Sediment grain density rho_s

inputs.name: water_density_kg_m3
inputs.description: Water density rho_w

inputs.name: porosity
inputs.description: In-situ sediment porosity p

outputs.description: Wave energy flux at breaking (E*Cg)_b in W/m

outputs.name: transport_rate_m3_yr
outputs.description: Volumetric longshore transport rate Q_l (m³/year, absolute value)

templates.description: Calculate exit gradient at downstream toe and factor of safety against piping
templates.long_description: Computes the exit hydraulic gradient at a dam or levee downstream toe using i_exit = delta_h / L_seepage, and the critical gradient for piping initiation from i_cr = (G_s - 1) / (1 + e), per USACE EM 1110-2-1901. Derives the factor of safety against piping and the saturated and buoyant unit weights of the foundation soil. Used in dam safety and geotechnical seepage analysis.

inputs.name: head_difference_m
inputs.description: Head difference across the structure (upstream pool minus tailwater level)

inputs.name: seepage_path_length_m
inputs.description: Total seepage path length through the foundation from upstream to downstream toe

inputs.name: specific_gravity
inputs.description: Specific gravity of foundation soil solids G_s

inputs.name: void_ratio
inputs.description: Void ratio of the foundation soil e

inputs.name: foundation_soil_type
inputs.description: Foundation soil classification

outputs.name: exit_gradient
outputs.description: Exit gradient at the downstream toe i_exit (dimensionless)

outputs.name: critical_gradient
outputs.description: Critical hydraulic gradient for piping initiation i_cr (dimensionless)

outputs.name: factor_of_safety
outputs.description: Factor of safety against piping FoS = i_cr / i_exit (dimensionless)

outputs.name: saturated_unit_weight_kn_m3
outputs.description: Saturated unit weight of foundation soil gamma_sat (kN/m3)

outputs.name: buoyant_unit_weight_kn_m3
outputs.description: Buoyant (submerged) unit weight of foundation soil gamma_b (kN/m3)

templates.description: Calculate uplift pressure distribution on concrete gravity dam foundation for stability analysis
templates.long_description: Calculates the bilinear uplift pressure distribution beneath a concrete gravity dam using the simplified method from USACE EM 1110-2-2200. Computes pressures at the upstream face, drainage gallery, and downstream face, then integrates the trapezoidal distribution to obtain total uplift force per unit length for sliding and overturning stability checks.

inputs.name: headwater_depth_m
inputs.description: Headwater depth (hydraulic head) upstream of the dam

inputs.name: tailwater_depth_m
inputs.description: Tailwater depth (hydraulic head) downstream of the dam

inputs.name: base_width_m
inputs.description: Total base width of the dam from upstream to downstream face

inputs.name: drain_distance_m
inputs.description: Distance from the upstream face to the drainage gallery line

inputs.name: drain_efficiency_pct
inputs.description: Drainage gallery efficiency as a percentage (0 = no drain, 100 = full relief)

outputs.name: upstream_pressure_kpa
outputs.description: Uplift pressure at the upstream face P_upstream (kPa)

outputs.name: drain_pressure_kpa
outputs.description: Uplift pressure at the drain line P_drain (kPa)

outputs.name: downstream_pressure_kpa
outputs.description: Uplift pressure at the downstream face P_downstream (kPa)

outputs.name: total_uplift_force_kn_m
outputs.description: Total uplift force per unit length of dam U (kN/m)

templates.description: Intersection sight distance for give-way and stop-controlled intersections per AGRD Part 4A
templates.long_description: Calculates the required intersection sight distance (ISD) along the major road at unsignalised intersections using ISD = V × t_gap / 3.6 per AGRD Part 4A. Adjusts the base gap acceptance time for control type, vehicle class, approach grade, and number of crossing lanes, then derives sight-triangle dimensions for clearance verification.

inputs.name: design_speed_kmh
inputs.description: Design speed of the major road V

inputs.name: control_type
inputs.description: Traffic control on the minor approach

inputs.name: approach_grade_pct
inputs.description: Grade of the minor-road approach (positive = upgrade from intersection)

inputs.name: num_lanes_to_cross
inputs.description: Number of lanes on the major road the minor-road vehicle must cross

inputs.name: vehicle_type
inputs.description: Design vehicle entering from the minor road

inputs.name: setback_distance_m
inputs.description: Distance from major-road edge to driver eye on the minor approach

outputs.name: gap_time_s
outputs.description: Total gap acceptance time including all corrections (s)

outputs.name: required_isd_m
outputs.description: Required intersection sight distance along the major road (m)

outputs.name: sight_triangle_major_m
outputs.description: Sight triangle leg along the major road (m)

outputs.name: sight_triangle_minor_m
outputs.description: Sight triangle leg along the minor road (m)

templates.description: Stopping sight distance on a longitudinal grade using d_r + d_b per AGRD Part 3 §5
templates.long_description: Computes stopping sight distance (SSD) on graded road segments as the sum of reaction distance d_r = V*t_r/3.6 and braking distance d_b = V^2/(254*(f+g)), using speed-dependent longitudinal friction coefficients from AGRD Part 3 Table 5.5. Accounts for uphill and downhill grades to verify geometric design adequacy for safe vehicle stopping.

inputs.name: design_speed_km_h
inputs.description: Design speed V

inputs.name: grade_pct
inputs.description: Longitudinal grade (positive = uphill, negative = downhill)

inputs.name: reaction_time_s
inputs.description: Driver reaction time t_r

templates.description: Calculate factor of safety for upstream slope during rapid reservoir drawdown
templates.long_description: Computes the factor of safety before and after rapid reservoir drawdown on an embankment dam upstream slope using the simplified infinite slope method per USACE EM 1110-2-1902. Assumes undrained pore-pressure response in low-permeability core materials, comparing the submerged steady-state condition to the post-drawdown condition where full saturated weight drives shear but pore pressures remain elevated.

inputs.name: slope_angle_deg
inputs.description: Upstream slope angle measured from horizontal

inputs.name: slip_depth_m
inputs.description: Depth of potential slip surface below the slope face

inputs.name: cohesion_kpa
inputs.description: Effective cohesion c' of the embankment material

inputs.name: friction_angle_deg
inputs.description: Effective friction angle phi' of the embankment material

inputs.name: saturated_unit_weight_kn_m3
inputs.description: Saturated unit weight of the embankment material gamma_sat

inputs.name: initial_reservoir_level_m
inputs.description: Initial (pre-drawdown) reservoir water level above the dam base

inputs.name: final_reservoir_level_m
inputs.description: Final (post-drawdown) reservoir water level above the dam base

outputs.name: fos_before_drawdown
outputs.description: Factor of safety of the upstream slope before drawdown (submerged steady state)

outputs.name: fos_after_drawdown
outputs.description: Factor of safety of the upstream slope after rapid drawdown (undrained pore pressures)

outputs.name: pore_pressure_kpa
outputs.description: Undrained pore pressure at the slip surface after drawdown u (kPa)

templates.description: Factor of safety under pseudo-static seismic loading using the infinite slope method
templates.long_description: Computes the factor of safety for embankment or natural slopes subjected to earthquake inertia forces using the pseudo-static infinite slope method per USACE EM 1110-2-1902 and ICOLD Bulletin 148. Applies horizontal and vertical seismic coefficients to the sliding mass, accounts for pore water pressure via the pore pressure ratio ru, and derives the yield acceleration ky at which FoS reaches unity.

inputs.name: slope_angle_deg
inputs.description: Slope angle beta measured from horizontal

inputs.name: slip_depth_m
inputs.description: Depth to the slip surface measured vertically from the slope face

inputs.name: cohesion_kpa
inputs.description: Effective cohesion c' of the embankment material

inputs.name: friction_angle_deg
inputs.description: Effective friction angle phi' of the embankment material

inputs.name: unit_weight_kn_m3
inputs.description: Bulk unit weight of the embankment material gamma

inputs.name: pore_pressure_ratio
inputs.description: Pore pressure ratio ru = u / (gamma * z * cos²β)

inputs.name: kh
inputs.description: Horizontal seismic coefficient (fraction of g)

inputs.name: kv
inputs.description: Vertical seismic coefficient (fraction of g, acts upward for conservative case)

outputs.name: fos
outputs.description: Factor of safety under pseudo-static seismic loading (dimensionless)

outputs.name: yield_acceleration_ky
outputs.description: Yield (critical) horizontal acceleration at which FoS = 1.0 (fraction of g)

templates.description: Factor of safety for embankment slope under steady-state seepage
templates.long_description: Computes the factor of safety against shallow translational failure on an embankment dam slope under steady-state seepage using the infinite slope method. The pore pressure ratio (ru) represents the phreatic surface location, and the balance of driving shear stress against Mohr-Coulomb resisting strength along the failure plane determines stability. Applicable to preliminary dam safety assessments per USACE EM 1110-2-1902 and ANCOLD guidelines.

inputs.name: slope_angle_deg
inputs.description: Embankment slope angle beta measured from horizontal

inputs.name: failure_depth_m
inputs.description: Depth from slope surface to the failure plane z

inputs.name: cohesion_kpa
inputs.description: Effective cohesion of embankment fill c'

inputs.name: friction_angle_deg
inputs.description: Effective friction angle of embankment fill phi'

inputs.name: saturated_unit_weight_kn_m3
inputs.description: Saturated unit weight of embankment fill gamma_sat

inputs.name: pore_pressure_ratio
inputs.description: Pore pressure ratio ru = u / (gamma_sat * z) representing steady-state phreatic surface

outputs.name: fos
outputs.description: Factor of safety against slope failure FoS (dimensionless)

outputs.name: driving_stress_kpa
outputs.description: Driving shear stress along the failure plane tau_d (kPa)

outputs.name: resisting_stress_kpa
outputs.description: Resisting shear stress along the failure plane tau_r (kPa)

templates.description: Rankine lateral earth pressure with water table effects for retaining wall design
templates.long_description: Calculates active and passive earth pressure coefficients and resultant forces on retaining walls using Rankine theory per AS 4678. Handles two-zone pressure distribution when a water table is present, computing effective earth pressure above and below the water table plus hydrostatic thrust. Outputs include Ka, Kp, active and passive forces, overturning moment, and water force per unit wall length for stability assessment.

inputs.name: wall_height_m
inputs.description: Retaining wall height H

inputs.name: friction_angle_deg
inputs.description: Effective friction angle of backfill soil phi'

inputs.name: cohesion_kpa
inputs.description: Effective cohesion of backfill soil c'

inputs.name: unit_weight_kn_m3
inputs.description: Total (bulk) unit weight of backfill soil gamma

inputs.name: surcharge_kpa
inputs.description: Uniform surcharge pressure on the backfill surface q

inputs.name: water_table_depth_m
inputs.description: Depth from ground surface to the water table behind the wall (equal to wall height means no water)

inputs.name: backfill_slope_deg
inputs.description: Backfill slope angle beta above horizontal

outputs.name: active_force_kn_per_m
outputs.description: Total active earth pressure force per unit wall length Pa (kN/m)

outputs.name: passive_force_kn_per_m
outputs.description: Total passive earth pressure force per unit wall length Pp (kN/m)

outputs.name: active_moment_knm_per_m
outputs.description: Active overturning moment about the wall base Ma (kNm/m)

outputs.name: water_force_kn_per_m
outputs.description: Hydrostatic water force per unit wall length Pw (kN/m)

templates.description: Sliding, overturning, and bearing stability checks for gravity retaining walls
templates.long_description: Evaluates the external stability of a rectangular gravity retaining wall against three failure modes: sliding along the base, overturning about the toe, and bearing capacity failure of the foundation soil. Active earth pressure is computed using Rankine theory for horizontal backfill, and bearing capacity uses Terzaghi strip footing factors. Outputs include factors of safety, eccentricity, and maximum base pressure per AS 4678 and Eurocode 7.

inputs.name: wall_height_m
inputs.description: Total height of the gravity retaining wall H

inputs.name: base_width_m
inputs.description: Width of the wall base B (typically 0.5H to 0.7H)

inputs.name: wall_thickness_m
inputs.description: Thickness of the wall stem at the top t

inputs.name: concrete_unit_weight_kn_m3
inputs.description: Unit weight of the wall material (concrete or masonry) gamma_c

inputs.name: backfill_friction_angle_deg
inputs.description: Effective friction angle of the backfill soil phi'

inputs.name: backfill_unit_weight_kn_m3
inputs.description: Total unit weight of the backfill soil gamma_s

inputs.name: backfill_cohesion_kpa
inputs.description: Effective cohesion of the backfill soil c'

inputs.name: surcharge_kpa
inputs.description: Uniform surcharge pressure on the backfill surface q

inputs.name: foundation_friction_angle_deg
inputs.description: Effective friction angle of the foundation soil phi_f

inputs.name: foundation_cohesion_kpa
inputs.description: Effective cohesion of the foundation soil c_f

inputs.name: base_friction_ratio
inputs.description: Ratio of base interface friction to foundation soil friction (typically 2/3)

outputs.name: ka
outputs.description: Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka (dimensionless)

outputs.name: fos_sliding
outputs.description: Factor of safety against sliding along the base (dimensionless)

outputs.name: fos_overturning
outputs.description: Factor of safety against overturning about the toe (dimensionless)

outputs.name: fos_bearing
outputs.description: Factor of safety against bearing capacity failure (dimensionless)

outputs.name: eccentricity_m
outputs.description: Eccentricity of the resultant force from the base centre e (m)

outputs.name: max_base_pressure_kpa
outputs.description: Maximum base contact pressure under the footing q_max (kPa)

templates.description: Calculate spillway discharge capacity using the standard weir equation with pier/abutment corrections
templates.long_description: Calculates spillway discharge capacity using the weir equation Q = C*L_eff*H_e^1.5 with pier and abutment contraction corrections to effective crest length and approach velocity head adjustments per USBR Design Standard No. 14 and USACE EM 1110-2-1603. Supports ogee and broad-crested weir types for dam safety and flood routing assessments.

inputs.name: crest_length_m
inputs.description: Gross crest length of the spillway L

inputs.name: design_head_m
inputs.description: Design head over the spillway crest H

inputs.name: discharge_coefficient
inputs.description: Weir discharge coefficient C (SI metric)

inputs.name: number_of_piers
inputs.description: Number of piers on the spillway crest N

inputs.name: pier_shape
inputs.description: Pier nose geometry for contraction coefficient Kp

inputs.name: abutment_shape
inputs.description: Abutment geometry for contraction coefficient Ka

inputs.name: approach_channel_width_m
inputs.description: Width of the approach channel upstream of the spillway B

inputs.name: approach_depth_m
inputs.description: Depth of flow in the approach channel h_approach

outputs.name: effective_crest_length_m
outputs.description: Effective crest length after pier and abutment contraction corrections L_eff (m)

outputs.name: total_energy_head_m
outputs.description: Total energy head over crest He = H + Va²/(2g) (m)

outputs.name: unit_discharge_m3_s_per_m
outputs.description: Unit discharge per metre of effective crest q (m³/s/m)

templates.description: Estimate stilling basin length required for energy dissipation based on Froude number and USBR basin type
templates.long_description: Estimates the required stilling basin length for energy dissipation downstream of a spillway using USBR hydraulic design methods. Calculates the entry Froude number, derives the sequent (conjugate) depth from the Belanger equation d2 = (d1/2)*(sqrt(1+8*Fr^2)-1), then selects the appropriate USBR basin type (I, II, or III) and corresponding basin length factor.
standards: USBR Hydraulic Design of Stilling Basins

inputs.name: unit_discharge_m3_s_m
inputs.description: Unit discharge at the spillway toe q (discharge per metre width)

inputs.name: drop_height_m
inputs.description: Vertical drop height from reservoir level to basin floor ΔH

inputs.name: tailwater_depth_m
inputs.description: Tailwater depth downstream of the basin d_tw

outputs.name: froude_number
outputs.description: Froude number at basin entry Fr₁

outputs.name: sequent_depth_m
outputs.description: Sequent (conjugate) depth from Belanger equation d₂ (m)

outputs.name: basin_type
outputs.description: USBR basin type code (0.0 = none, 1.0 = Type I, 2.0 = Type II, 3.0 = Type III)

templates.description: Hydraulic grade line check for a single stormwater pipe reach
templates.long_description: Computes the hydraulic grade line (HGL) at the upstream pit of a single stormwater pipe reach using Manning's equation for friction loss and a pit loss coefficient for junction losses. Checks whether the HGL remains below the pit surface level with adequate clearance to prevent surcharging, following ARR 2019 and local council DCP methods.

inputs.name: design_flow_m3_per_s
inputs.description: Design peak flow Q at the pipe reach

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_mm
inputs.description: Nominal internal pipe diameter DN

inputs.name: pipe_length_m
inputs.description: Pipe reach length L between pits

inputs.name: mannings_n
inputs.description: Manning's roughness coefficient n for the pipe material

inputs.name: pit_loss_coefficient
inputs.description: Pit/junction loss coefficient K (dimensionless)

inputs.name: tailwater_level_m
inputs.description: HGL at the downstream pit (tailwater condition)

inputs.name: surface_level_m
inputs.description: Surface level at the upstream pit

inputs.name: minimum_clearance_mm
inputs.description: Minimum required clearance between HGL and surface level

outputs.name: friction_loss_m
outputs.description: Friction head loss h_f through the pipe (m)

outputs.name: hgl_upstream_m
outputs.description: HGL elevation at the upstream pit (m AHD)

outputs.name: clearance_mm
outputs.description: Clearance between surface level and HGL (mm), positive = below surface

outputs.name: surcharge_ratio
outputs.description: HGL to surface level ratio (> 1.0 indicates surcharging)

outputs.name: pass_fail
outputs.description: Surcharge compliance (1.0 = pass, clearance adequate; 0.0 = fail)

templates.description: Downstream pipe invert level calculation for stormwater drainage
templates.long_description: Calculates the downstream invert level, obvert (crown) level, cover depth, and grade fall for a stormwater drainage pipe given upstream invert, length, grade, diameter, and downstream surface level. Checks cover adequacy against minimum requirements for the installation context per Australian local-authority stormwater drainage standards.

inputs.name: upstream_invert_m
inputs.description: Upstream invert level IL_us

inputs.name: pipe_length_m
inputs.description: Pipe length between pits L

inputs.name: pipe_grade_percent
inputs.description: Pipe longitudinal grade as a percentage

inputs.name: pipe_diameter_mm
inputs.description: Nominal pipe internal diameter D

inputs.name: surface_level_ds_m
inputs.description: Surface level at downstream pit

inputs.name: minimum_cover_mm
inputs.description: Minimum required cover depth over pipe crown

outputs.name: obvert_level_m
outputs.description: Obvert (crown) level at downstream end OL_ds (m AHD)

templates.description: Size roof downpipes per AS/NZS 3500.3 using catchment area and rainfall intensity
templates.long_description: Sizes roof downpipes by calculating the design stormwater flow per downpipe from the catchment area and rainfall intensity, then selecting the smallest standard uPVC round diameter whose full-bore capacity meets or exceeds the demand per AS/NZS 3500.3 Table 4.3. Used in building plumbing and roof drainage design to ensure adequate stormwater disposal from roofs.

inputs.name: roof_catchment_area_m2
inputs.description: Effective roof catchment area served by each group of downpipes

inputs.name: rainfall_intensity_mm_hr
inputs.description: Design rainfall intensity for the ARI and duration

inputs.name: num_downpipes
inputs.description: Number of downpipes draining the catchment area

outputs.name: selected_capacity_l_s
outputs.description: Full-bore capacity of the selected downpipe (L/s)

outputs.name: compliance
outputs.description: Compliance flag: 1.0 if capacity >= design flow, else 0.0

templates.description: Size eaves gutters per AS/NZS 3500.3 using catchment area, rainfall intensity, and gutter grade
templates.long_description: Sizes eaves gutters by computing the design stormwater flow from the roof catchment area and rainfall intensity, then selecting the smallest standard gutter profile whose capacity (scaled from the AS/NZS 3500.3 Table 4.2 reference grade by the square root of the installed grade ratio) meets or exceeds the demand. Used in building roof drainage design to prevent overflow and water damage.

inputs.name: roof_catchment_area_m2
inputs.description: Effective roof catchment area draining to the gutter run

inputs.name: rainfall_intensity_mm_hr
inputs.description: Design rainfall intensity for the ARI and duration

inputs.name: gutter_profile
inputs.description: Nominated eaves gutter profile

inputs.name: gutter_grade_pct
inputs.description: Longitudinal grade of the gutter (percentage)

outputs.name: design_flow_l_s
outputs.description: Design stormwater flow from the catchment (L/s)

outputs.name: gutter_capacity_l_s
outputs.description: Capacity of the selected gutter at the installed grade (L/s)

outputs.name: required_gutter_size_mm
outputs.description: Nominal size of the smallest adequate standard gutter (mm)

outputs.name: compliance
outputs.description: Compliance flag: 1.0 if capacity >= design flow, else 0.0

templates.description: Rail cant (superelevation) and cant deficiency for curved track using E_eq = 11.82 * V^2 / R
templates.long_description: Computes equilibrium cant, cant deficiency, and maximum allowable speed for curved railway track sections using the ARTC ETS-05-00 / AREMA formula E_eq = C * V^2 / R. Supports both standard and narrow gauge constants, and is used in track geometry design to balance passenger comfort against derailment risk on curves.

inputs.name: max_cant_deficiency_mm
inputs.description: Maximum allowable cant deficiency C_d_max

inputs.name: gauge_type
inputs.description: Track gauge classification

templates.description: Minimum transition spiral length from cant runoff, cant deficiency rate of change, and twist criteria per ARTC ETS-05-00 / AREMA
templates.long_description: Determines the governing minimum transition spiral length at rail curve entries by evaluating three independent criteria: cant runoff rate, cant deficiency rate of change, and twist limit. The maximum of L_cant, L_cd, and L_twist governs, ensuring passenger comfort and track stability per ARTC ETS-05-00 and AREMA Chapter 5.

inputs.name: actual_cant_mm
inputs.description: Applied (actual) cant E_a

inputs.name: cant_deficiency_mm

inputs.name: rate_of_change_cd_mm_s
inputs.description: Maximum rate of change of cant deficiency D_cd

inputs.name: min_twist_ratio
inputs.description: Minimum twist ratio (e.g. 400 means 1 mm cant per 400 mm length)

outputs.name: spiral_length_cant_m
outputs.description: Minimum spiral length from cant runoff criterion (m)

outputs.name: spiral_length_cd_m
outputs.description: Minimum spiral length from cant deficiency rate criterion (m)

outputs.name: spiral_length_twist_m
outputs.description: Minimum spiral length from twist rate criterion (m)

templates.description: Minimum vertical curve radius and length for railway grade transitions using R_v = V^2 / (3.6^2 * a_v)
templates.long_description: Computes the minimum vertical curve radius and length required at railway grade transition points using R_v = V^2 / (12.96 * a_v) and L_v = (A/100) * R_v. Ensures vertical acceleration remains within passenger comfort and rolling stock safety limits per ARTC ETS-05-00 and AREMA Chapter 5.

inputs.name: initial_grade_pct
inputs.description: Initial longitudinal grade g1 (positive = uphill)

inputs.name: final_grade_pct
inputs.description: Final longitudinal grade g2 (positive = uphill)

inputs.name: design_speed_km_h
inputs.description: Design operating speed V

inputs.name: max_vertical_acceleration_m_s2
inputs.description: Maximum acceptable vertical acceleration a_v for passenger comfort

templates.description: Estimate annual pollutant loads from a catchment using the EMC method
templates.long_description: Estimates annual Total Suspended Solids (TSS), Total Phosphorus (TP), and Total Nitrogen (TN) pollutant loads from urban catchments using the Event Mean Concentration (EMC) method. Computes annual runoff volume as V = C*P*A*10 and multiplies by EMC values per MUSIC guidelines and Australian Runoff Quality to support water-sensitive urban design.

inputs.name: catchment_area_ha
inputs.description: Contributing catchment area A

inputs.name: annual_rainfall_mm
inputs.description: Mean annual rainfall P

inputs.name: runoff_coefficient
inputs.description: Volumetric runoff coefficient C (fraction of rainfall that becomes runoff)

inputs.name: emc_tss_mg_l
inputs.description: Event mean concentration of Total Suspended Solids

inputs.name: emc_tp_mg_l
inputs.description: Event mean concentration of Total Phosphorus

inputs.name: emc_tn_mg_l
inputs.description: Event mean concentration of Total Nitrogen

templates.description: Linear (Airy) wave theory: wavelength, celerity, and group velocity via dispersion relation (USACE CEM)
templates.long_description: Solves the linear wave dispersion relation using Newton-Raphson iteration to compute wavelength, phase celerity, and group velocity for monochromatic waves. Also derives wave steepness and relative depth (d/L) for water depth classification. Follows the USACE Coastal Engineering Manual Part II Chapter 1 formulation applicable across deep, intermediate, and shallow water regimes.

inputs.name: wave_period_s
inputs.description: Wave period T

inputs.name: water_depth_m
inputs.description: Water depth d

inputs.name: wave_height_m
inputs.description: Wave height H

outputs.name: wave_celerity_m_per_s
outputs.description: Wave phase celerity C = L / T (m/s)

outputs.name: relative_depth
outputs.description: Relative depth d/L (dimensionless); >0.5 deep, <0.05 shallow, else intermediate

templates.description: Wave breaking criteria using depth-limited breaking height, Iribarren number, and breaker type classification (USACE CEM)
templates.long_description: Evaluates wave breaking conditions using the Weggel (1972) breaker depth index gamma_b to compute depth-limited breaking wave height H_b = gamma_b * d, then classifies breaker type (spilling, plunging, or surging) via the Iribarren surf similarity parameter. Used in coastal structure design and nearshore hazard assessment per the USACE Coastal Engineering Manual.

inputs.name: wave_height_m
inputs.description: Incident (deep-water) wave height H_0

inputs.name: wave_period_s
inputs.description: Wave period T

inputs.name: water_depth_m
inputs.description: Water depth at location of interest d

inputs.name: bottom_slope
inputs.description: Bottom slope m (rise/run ratio, dimensionless)

templates.description: Wave shoaling and refraction using Fenton & McKee (1990) explicit wavelength approximation and Snell's law (USACE CEM)
templates.long_description: Transforms deep-water wave height to nearshore conditions by computing shoaling coefficient K_s and refraction coefficient K_r, yielding H = H_0 * K_s * K_r. Uses the Fenton and McKee (1990) explicit dispersion relation approximation to avoid iterative wavelength solutions, and applies Snell's law for wave angle refraction per the USACE Coastal Engineering Manual.

inputs.name: deep_water_wave_height_m
inputs.description: Deep-water significant wave height H_0

inputs.name: wave_period_s
inputs.description: Wave period T

inputs.name: nearshore_depth_m
inputs.description: Water depth at the nearshore location d

inputs.name: deep_water_wave_angle_deg
inputs.description: Deep-water wave crest angle to bottom contour theta_0

templates.description: 2% exceedance wave runup on coastal structures using EurOtop (2018) TAW formula
templates.long_description: Calculates the 2% exceedance wave runup height Ru2% on sloped coastal structures using the EurOtop (2018) TAW formula, which evaluates both breaking and surging runup expressions and takes the governing (minimum) value. Accounts for structure slope, surface roughness, and berm geometry to determine freeboard requirements for dikes, seawalls, and breakwaters.

inputs.name: wave_height_m
inputs.description: Significant spectral wave height H_m0

inputs.name: wave_period_s

templates.description: Calculate design wind pressure from wind speed and aerodynamic factors per AS/NZS 1170.2
templates.long_description: Computes design wind pressure on a building surface using the AS/NZS 1170.2 Section 2.4 formula p = 0.5 * rho_air * V_des^2 * C_fig * C_dyn. Determines the basic dynamic pressure from wind speed, applies aerodynamic shape and dynamic response factors, and calculates the total wind force on a tributary area for structural design.

inputs.name: design_wind_speed_m_per_s
inputs.description: Design wind speed V_des,theta for the chosen cardinal direction

inputs.name: cfig
inputs.description: Aerodynamic shape factor C_fig (positive = pressure, negative = suction)

inputs.name: cdyn
inputs.description: Dynamic response factor C_dyn (1.0 for most low-rise structures)

inputs.name: air_density_kg_per_m3

inputs.name: tributary_area_m2

outputs.description: Basic dynamic wind pressure q = 0.5 * rho * V^2 (kPa)

outputs.name: design_pressure_kpa
outputs.description: Design wind pressure p = q * C_fig * C_dyn (kPa)

outputs.name: total_force_kn
outputs.description: Total wind force on the tributary area F = p * A (kN)

templates.description: Site wind speed V_sit,beta from regional speed and multipliers per AS/NZS 1170.2
templates.long_description: Calculates the design site wind speed using the AS/NZS 1170.2 Section 2.2 equation V_sit,beta = V_R * M_d * M_z,cat * M_s * M_t. The terrain/height multiplier M_z,cat is looked up from Table 4.1(A) with linear interpolation for intermediate heights across five terrain categories. Applicable to buildings and structures in Australian and New Zealand wind regions.

inputs.name: wind_direction_multiplier

templates.description: Wind loads on ground-mounted solar PV arrays including uplift, downforce, and drag
templates.long_description: Calculates wind loads on ground-mounted photovoltaic arrays by combining AS/NZS 1170.2 dynamic pressure with SEAOC PV2-2017 net pressure coefficients. Interpolates GCrn values for tilt angle and row position to determine uplift suction, downward pressure, per-module uplift force, and horizontal drag force per metre of array width. Applicable to fixed-tilt utility and commercial solar installations in Australian wind regions.

templates.description: Arc flash incident energy calculation per IEEE 1584 and NFPA 70E
templates.long_description: Calculates arc flash incident energy, arcing current, arc flash boundary distance, and required PPE category using the IEEE 1584-2002 empirical method for systems from 208 V to 15 kV. Models both low-voltage and medium-voltage arcing current equations and normalized energy formulas, supporting electrical safety assessments and arc flash labelling per NFPA 70E.

templates.description: Battery energy storage system sizing calculation per IEC 62933
templates.long_description: Determines the beginning-of-life installed capacity for a battery energy storage system using E_bol = (P * t) / (DoD * eta_rt * (1 - degradation)), accounting for depth of discharge, round-trip efficiency, and end-of-life degradation. Supports grid-scale peaking, renewable firming, and microgrid applications per IEC 62933 methodology.

templates.description: Busbar short-circuit electromagnetic force and stress calculation per IEEE 605 / IEC 60865-1
templates.long_description: Calculates the electromagnetic force per unit length on the centre phase of a three-phase flat busbar arrangement during a symmetrical short circuit using Fm = (mu_0 / 2pi) * (sqrt(3)/2) * ip^2 / a, then derives peak span force and bending stress. Used to verify busbar mechanical adequacy in switchgear and substation design per IEEE 605 and IEC 60865-1.

templates.description: Cable ampacity derating calculation per AS/NZS 3008.1.1

Covers XLPE and PVC insulation across buried, tray, conduit, and air installations per AS/NZS 3008.1.1 and IEC 60287.

templates.description: Cable voltage drop calculation per AS/NZS 3008.1.1
templates.long_description: Calculates voltage drop along a cable run using tabulated mV/A/m values from AS/NZS 3008.1.1 for copper and aluminium multicore cables, adjusted for power factor and circuit type. Checks compliance against the AS/NZS 3000 five-percent limit to verify that voltage at the load stays within acceptable limits for equipment operation.

templates.description: Single span sag-tension calculation for overhead contact wires per EN 50119
templates.long_description: Computes mid-span sag, wire length, and catenary constant for a single level span of overhead contact wire used in rail electrification. Applies both the parabolic approximation (S = wL^2/8T) and exact catenary equations per EN 50119, enabling comparison of the two methods across light rail, mainline, and high-speed catenary scenarios.

templates.description: Control valve Cv sizing for incompressible liquid service per ISA-75.01.01
templates.long_description: Sizes control valves for incompressible liquid service by computing the required flow coefficient Cv from the ISA-75.01.01 equation Kv = Q * sqrt(SG / deltaP_eff), with Cv = 1.156 * Kv. Checks for choked (cavitating) flow using the liquid pressure recovery factor FL and critical pressure ratio factor FF, essential for process control and piping system design.

templates.description: Solar PV DC/AC ratio (inverter loading ratio) and energy yield calculation
templates.long_description: Calculates the DC/AC ratio (inverter loading ratio) for solar PV systems and estimates annual energy yield accounting for system losses, inverter efficiency, and clipping losses from inverter overloading. templates.description: Steady-state thermal rating (ampacity) of bare overhead conductors per IEEE 738

templates.description: Factor of safety for infinite slope failure
templates.long_description: Computes the factor of safety against shallow planar failure on long uniform slopes using the infinite slope equation with Mohr-Coulomb shear strength. Accounts for pore water pressure from a water table parallel to the slope surface, balancing the driving shear stress (gamma*z*sin(beta)*cos(beta)) against the resisting cohesion and frictional strength along the failure plane.

templates.description: CPT parameter derivation using Robertson (1990) correlations
templates.long_description: Derives geotechnical design parameters from cone penetration test (CPTu) data using the Robertson (1990) classification framework. Computes corrected cone resistance qt, normalized parameters Qt and Fr, soil behavior type index Ic, and estimates undrained shear strength (via Nkt) for clay-like soils or friction angle for sand-like soils based on the Ic = 2.6 boundary.

templates.description: SPT N-value corrections using Liao & Whitman (1986)
templates.long_description: Applies standard corrections to raw SPT blow counts following the Liao and Whitman (1986) procedure per ASTM D1586 practice. Applies energy (CE), borehole diameter (CB), sampler (CS), and rod length (CR) corrections to obtain N60, then normalizes to a reference overburden pressure of 100 kPa using CN = sqrt(Pa/sigma'v) to produce the corrected (N1)60 value for liquefaction and strength correlations.
